minutes_briefs.title: BC Firearm Violence Prevention rules take effect October 1, 2026
minutes_briefs.summary: The BC government has made a new regulation under the Firearm Violence Prevention Act. This regulation sets rules for how low-velocity firearms (like some air guns or pellet guns) and imitation firearms (fake guns that look real) must be transported, carried, or stored in vehicles and boats. The rules take effect on October 1, 2026. The regulation also defines what counts as a 'vehicle' for these rules, including motorized devices and sleighs or wagons.
minutes_briefs.action_required: Review how your business transports or stores low-velocity or imitation firearms in vehicles or boats and prepare to comply by October 1, 2026.

key_dates.label: BC Firearm Violence Prevention General Regulation takes effect

minutes_briefs.source_body:
Regulation: B.C. Reg. 64/2026
Made under: Firearm Violence Prevention Act
Published in: No. 8 – B.C. Regs. 62 to 76, May 5, 2026
Deposit date: April 27, 2026

B.C. Reg. 64/2026, deposited April 27, 2026, under theFIREARM VIOLENCE PREVENTION ACT[sections 79, 80, 81 and 85]. Order in Council 157/2026, approved and ordered April 27, 2026. On the recommendation of the undersigned, the Lieutenant Governor, by and with the advice and consent of the Executive Council, orders that, effective October 1, 2026, the attached Firearm Violence Prevention General Regulation is made. — N. KRIEGER,Minister of Public Safety and Solicitor General; N. SHARMA,Presiding Member of the Executive Council. FIREARM VIOLENCE PREVENTION GENERAL REGULATION "Act"means theFirearm Violence Prevention Act; "Canadian Forces"means the Canadian Forces as defined in section 2 of theCriminal Code; "police service"means any of the following within the meaning of thePolice Act: (a)	the provincial police service; (b)	a municipal police department; (c)	a designated policing unit. Descriptors for federal enactments 2In this regulation, if a reference to a federal enactment is followed by italicized text in square brackets that describes the subject matter of the federal enactment, the text in square brackets (a)	is not part of this regulation, and (b)	is to be considered to have been added editorially for convenience of reference only. Part 2 – Matters Prescribed for Provisions of Act 3The following devices are prescribed as vehicles for the purposes of paragraph (h) of the definition of"vehicle"in section 1 of the Act: (a)	a designated motorized device as defined in section 1 of theMotor Vehicle Act; (b)	a sleigh or wagon that is not a vehicle as defined in section 1 of theMotor Vehicle Act. Requirements for transporting, carrying or storing in vehicles or boats 4(1)	For the purposes of section 4 (2) of the Act and unless subsection (2) of this section applies, if a low-velocity firearm or imitation firearm is being transported, carried or stored in or on a vehicle or boat,
